data.purpose: Nonces, gaps, multipliers, times, and replay values.

shadows.name: none
shadows.purpose: Default for command panels, tables, rails, and tier columns.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard focus only; not decorative elevation.

motion.name: live-pulse
motion.purpose: Live status dot only; do not animate large data regions.

components.name: Operator Button
components.description: Primary command action, used for destructive or high-intent operator actions such as Rotate Seed.
components.css: .ds-operator-button { background: #e3711f; color: #321200; border: 1px solid #000; border-radius: 0; padding: 8px 20px; font-family: Inter Tight, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.2em; text-transform: uppercase; transition: background-color 150ms ease-out, color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-operator-button:hover { background: #ffb68c; } .ds-operator-button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #e0e2eb; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Rail Icon Button
components.description: Square command-rail icon affordance with orange active state and hard position marker.
components.css: .ds-rail-button { position: relative; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 48px; height: 48px; border: 0; border-radius: 0; background: transparent; color: #c1c6d5; font-size: 28px; transition: color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-rail-button:hover { color: #e0e2eb; } .ds-rail-button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #e3711f; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-rail-button-active { color: #e3711f; } .ds-rail-marker { position: absolute; left: -16px; top: 50%; width: 4px; height: 24px; transform: translateY(-50%); background: #e3711f; } .ds-rail-icon { line-height: 1; }

components.name: Tier Command Column
components.description: Vertical multiplier tier column with hard borders, a compact header, and mono active gap value.
components.html:
1066X

4,377

ACTIVE GAP

--
components.css: .ds-tier-column { min-width: 170px; background: #0A0A0A; color: #e0e2eb; border-right: 1px solid #414753; border-radius: 0; font-family: Inter Tight, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-tier-column:hover { background: #111111; } .ds-tier-header { height: 80px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; border-bottom: 1px solid #414753; } .ds-tier-header h2 { margin: 0; color: #aac7ff; font-size: 20px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-tier-body { padding: 48px 20px 20px; text-align: center; } .ds-tier-value { margin: 0; color: #aac7ff; font-family: JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, monospace; font-size: 42px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: -0.025em; } .ds-tier-label { margin: 12px 0 0; color: #c1c6d5; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-tier-rule { margin: 48px 0 16px; border-top: 1px solid #414753; } .ds-tier-box { width: 75%; margin: 0 auto; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid rgba(65, 71, 83, 0.7); background: #111111; color: rgba(193, 198, 213, 0.3); font-family: JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, monospace; font-size: 24px; font-weight: 700; }

components.name: Stream Tape Row
components.description: Dense evidence row for nonce, result, gap, and time.
components.css: .ds-stream-row { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1.1fr 1fr 0.7fr 1fr; align-items: center; gap: 20px; padding: 20px; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(65, 71, 83, 0.4); background: #0A0A0A; color: #e0e2eb; font-family: JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, monospace; } .ds-stream-row:hover { background: #111111; } .ds-stream-nonce, .ds-stream-result { font-size: 18px; font-weight: 700; } .ds-stream-gap { color: rgba(193, 198, 213, 0.4); font-size: 18px; text-align: right; } .ds-stream-time { color: #c1c6d5; font-size: 12px; text-align: right; }

components.name: Telemetry Item
components.description: Compact top-bar status pair for node, nonce, duration, latency, and gap telemetry.
components.css: .ds-telemetry-item { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 16px; color: #e0e2eb; } .ds-telemetry-label { color: #c1c6d5; font-family: Inter Tight, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-telemetry-value { font-family: JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, monospace; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; } .ds-telemetry-live { color: #a3e635; }

components.name: Version Badge
components.description: Small square rail token for version or fixed system metadata.
components.css: .ds-version-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 40px; height: 40px; border: 1px solid #414753; border-radius: 0; background: #272a31; color: #e0e2eb; font-family: JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, monospace; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; }

narrative.overview: This design system is an operator console for deterministic fairness analysis: a black-grid command surface where every pixel should feel inspectable, logged, and accountable. The visual center is frontend/src/pages/LiveStreamDetail.tsx and the Stream Tape screenshot reference, not the older electric-blue app shell. Future surfaces should inherit the same hard panel divisions, compact telemetry, mono-heavy numbers, and signal-state color language.
keyCharacteristics: Full-screen command layout with black surfaces and hard grid lines.
Dense telemetry first: status, node, nonce, duration, gaps, stream tape.
Mono numerals for evidence values; condensed sans for labels and headers.
State color is rare, named, and meaningful: orange for command/action, green for live status, tier colors for thresholds.
Zero-radius panels, flat surfaces, and border-based depth.

rules.name: The Black Grid Rule
rules.body: Operator screens are built from black planes and hard 1px grid lines. Do not use soft cards to create structure.

rules.name: The Signal Rarity Rule
rules.body: Orange, green, and tier colors earn attention because they are rare. Do not spread them across inactive states or decorative backgrounds.

rules.name: The Label Plus Color Rule
rules.body: Never rely on color alone for status. Every status color needs text, icon, position, or shape support.

rules.name: The Evidence Mono Rule
rules.body: Nonces, seed identifiers, timings, gap counts, multipliers, and replay outputs use JetBrains Mono.

rules.name: The No-Friendly-Copy Rule
rules.body: Labels should be short, operational, and proof-oriented. Prefer NODE_ID, NONCE, GAPS, and ROTATE SEED over conversational UI copy.

rules.name: The No Float Rule
rules.body: Panels do not hover above the page. They lock into the grid.

rules.name: The Border Is Structure Rule
rules.body: Use 1px borders and shared edges to establish hierarchy. Do not pair borders with soft shadows.

dos: Do treat frontend/src/pages/LiveStreamDetail.tsx and the Stream Tape screenshot as the authoritative brand reference for live/operator work.
Do preserve the black-grid command layout, zero-radius panels, hard dividers, compact telemetry, and mono-heavy data.
Do use operator-orange for primary commands and active rail state, not as general decoration.
Do use green only for live/online/optimal status, and always pair it with explicit status text.
Do keep data tables dense, aligned, and readable for long analysis sessions.
Do provide keyboard-visible focus states and reduced-motion behavior for live updates, pulsing indicators, and ticking values.
donts: Don't drift into a generic SaaS admin panel, fintech dashboard, casino/gambling app, playful crypto meme tool, soft rounded consumer app, Discord bot panel, pastel AI startup interface, or friendly CRM.
Don't use large rounded cards, bubbly gradients, emoji-heavy states, friendly onboarding fluff, oversized whitespace, soft shadows, marketing illustrations, or casual copy.
Don't make color the only state signal. Status must also be readable through labels, icons, position, shape, or pattern.
Don't introduce soft glassmorphism, blur panels, decorative glows, or floating cards into the LiveStreamDetail visual language.
Don't use the older electric-blue shell as the default source of truth for operator surfaces unless a task explicitly targets that shell.
Don't auto-prepend live data in a way that causes scroll jumps; preserve operator control over evidence review.
